title: Double Standard in Episode 9
wikitext:
[]: Double Standard in Episode 9             Quote Post by GAP » Sat Mar 15, 2014 12:15 am A poster from another made an observation about Asuka in this episode, You know th epsiode where Shinji and Asuka have to synch to beat an angel that can split in two? And they can't because Asuka can't swallow her pride? There's a moment in there that really stands out to me, mainly because of the gross double standards it represents. In the scene where Hikari and Toji are at Misato's apartment with Shinji and Asuka, and Asuka is all angry and says that it's Shinji's fault that they can't synch. But then Shinji synchs up perfectly with Rei, showing that it's Asuka's fault. so then Asuka gets all mad, throws a tantrum, and runs out of the room. And then Hikari gets mad at Shinji , and asks HIM to APOLGIZE! Even though he did NOTHING WRONG! If anyone here has to apologize, it's Asuka. I honestly didn't understand why they kept trying after that point, when it became clear that it would be easier to have Shinji and Rei (instead of Asuka) beat the angel

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Mar 15, 2014 12:17 am Yep, it's just Hikari being irrational and leaping to a friend's(?) aid. Nothing more to it than that, in my opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Sat Mar 15, 2014 12:33 am It was more or less Asuka's fault as Rei and Shinji did work quite well together and she was being unreasonable.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Sat Mar 15, 2014 12:48 am I wouldn't judge the class rep too harshly. Perhaps from her perspective it appeared as though Shinji was making much more of a serious effort to match Rei's movements rather than Asuka's. I think she was mostly just trying to support her friend who she felt was very upset. Still, it would've been nice to maybe have a brief follow-up scene later where Hikari and Shinji get a chance to "clear the air", as it were. Actually, now that I think about it, any scene where those two characters have a conversation together might have been a nice addition to just about any episode.

[]:             Quote Post by #1 Hanabro » Sat Mar 15, 2014 3:27 am Ha, I was just thinking of this just the other day. Yeah, Asuka has too much pride in my opinion, and girls like that really frustrate me. So she'd rather die than be saved by "wonder girl"? When the real issue is she should be thankful that the angel was destroyed? ...Yeah, I have quite the list of complaints as far as Asuka is concerned. Slapping Rei also dropped her rank quite a few points for me.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Mar 15, 2014 5:38 am Well yes, having a gigantic ego to hide her insecurities, leading to her near-pathological inability to admit her wrongs was the whole point of her character arc and the downright spiral she fell into from Zeruel onward.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Mar 15, 2014 8:10 am Um, aren't we discussing Episode 9, not 11?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Mar 15, 2014 11:36 am ^ Yes. Fixed.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Mar 15, 2014 11:01 pm That, but then again Shinji is passive enough to let strong-willed individuals like Asuka and Hikari push him around. He's more or less always done what he's been told, and in this instance it was probably best that Hikari ordered that he go after her; otherwise they would have never had that conversation over that sandwich, which so neatly segued into them actually beginning to work together and get along. He could have just done nothing and stayed there instead of reaching out to her, but that would epitomize everything Asuka resented about him later on (ie, "you won't even hold me!"). She needs a reason to like him in the first place, and perhaps Hikari unknowingly played matchmaker and planted the seeds of their complex relationship with each other.

[]:             Quote Post by #1 Hanabro » Sun Mar 16, 2014 1:39 am I'm glad I'm not in Shinji's place. Cause I'd totally pass on Asuka and go for Rei, but she's like his mom.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Mar 16, 2014 1:49 am ElMariachi wrote: Well yes, having a gigantic ego to hide her insecurities, leading to her near-pathological inability to admit her wrongs was the whole point of her character arc and the downright spiral she fell into from Zeruel onward.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Sun Mar 16, 2014 4:51 am Asuka is Shinji in reverse, she pretends to self assured to hide her issues. Asuka was arrogant and she was setting herself up for failure.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Mar 16, 2014 1:01 pm From Asuka's perspective: 03 - Shinji killed it 04 - Shinji killed it 05 - Shinji killed it 06 - Shared kill with Shinji, but he took charge in the cockpit 07 - Shared kill with Shinji, though he probably could have done it with Rei more effectively 08 - Killed the angel, but only survived because of Shinji 09 - Shinji killed it, but at least it was her plan 10 - Killed the angel, but was definately upstaged by Shinji 11 - completely uninvolved 12 - Ran away, Shinji killed it and beat her in synch tests 13 - Defeated, Shinji killed it 14 - Defeated, Shinji killed it 15 - Defeated, Rei killed it 16 - Completely useless, Rei killed it 17 - Shinji beat 02 in a duel and killed the angel, her piloting replacement 18 - Put up a good fight but lost yet again (and to a squad of her replacements no less) Before Lilliel she could tell herself she was the best on paper and was at least was contributing, with her highlight being acting as 'squad leader' vs Materiel (9). But from Liliel (12) onward the story she told herself about herself wasn't convincing anymore and she was locked into the downward spiral, her every move a desperate gambit to escape the inevitable crash.

[]:             Quote Post by #1 Hanabro » Sun Mar 16, 2014 3:47 pm The inevitable crash is what comes to people who have pride like Asuka's. The pride is the issue, what we see in her attitude is only the results of it.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Sun Mar 16, 2014 5:37 pm Pride comes before a fall altgough I'll admit it that is suck when Asuka got mind raped in 22(?).

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Mar 16, 2014 6:57 pm #1 Hanabro wrote: The inevitable crash is what comes to people who have pride like Asuka's. The pride is the issue, what we see in her attitude is only the results of it.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Mar 16, 2014 7:54 pm Asuka having a double standard? Hikari helping to make the completely new transfer student feel comfortable? Actually, this isn’t out of character for any of the characters in this episode. It doesn’t bother me too much. Maybe Hikari was being a bit too harsh. I can see her telling Shinji to apologize to Asuka in order so that there can be some sort of compromise between the two. (Asuka probably wouldn’t have come back if Shinji didn’t compromise like that.) But Hikari being that harsh in her delivery does feel a tad out of place.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Mar 17, 2014 3:06 pm Dudes, dudes, Hikari has a) no clue about what happened over the last three days, like Asuka literally kicking Shinji around and generally being an uncooperative thickhead b) possibly a better insight into Shinji's perspective that we or Shinji have at this point, but at the same time doesn't know her that well yet... we don't exactly know, but with Asuka acting all sparklingly sweet at school... Yeah. We don't know how close they were at that point, but wether thy're already buddies (therefore giving Hikari a better insight) or less aquanted than the others (and therefore, Hikari only knows her faking innocent sweetness at school) the end result is the same... Hikari being a bit on the conservative/reserved side is nothing new. (See also her reaction to the matching clothes...) She also groups Shinji with the "stupid trio", so that might add some subtle previous antagonism. Also, you can't expect Shinji or Misato to see anything other than her being prideful if she actively creates this image, and has been doing so for very long. They're no professional shrinks, they're random clueless people who just happen to be there when no one else is. But maybe HIkari was in a position to know that it was more than this. As to why they kept trying, Asuka had somewhat calmed down by the time Shinji found her, and seeing that it was important to her, he probably didn't want to hurt her feelings. He saw a more tangible side to her, I guess. It's very much sold as what could look like a bonding moment at the first glance, and Shinji does seem to have taken it that way at the time (See that strategically placed smile on Shinji's face), they have a snack and she just decides to count them in and goes on about how "we" are going to pwn Misato and Rei, which is probably te only way she's even capable of accepting someone as an ally. It's still degrading in a way, but for someone with Shinji's level of self-respect, it's better than nothing - it looks like she might come to be less hostile to him, so that looks good, right? Although a very important detail is that he does not in any way get this antagonism toward Rei and Misato. Ep 11 is also a major instance of her trying to get him to chime in in basically gossiping about Rei while she's in the room... in some ways, she wants an overt comfirmation that he's her ally or possesion or something, but he's not really into bashing people and sort of tries to just not incur anyone's dislike, which is not necessarily a bad thing. Even if you argue that there's some level of comitment problem there, and there probably is, Asuka never suggsted she wanted his comitment in any way or gave some of her own, and him not wanting to bash people does go into it to some degree, it's not mutually exclusive/multilayered an complex. But it was, in the specific contex of ep 9, something like a truce, and there was no reason it couldn't get even better at this point, so, if she's agreed already, why turn her down? Unit Zero is still being repaired and EVA 02 would probably be the most effective with its main designated pilot, especially since the mission required those jumpy acrobatics.

[]: Re: Double Standard in Episode 9             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Mar 17, 2014 3:25 pm GAP wrote: Asuka was an arrogant twat who didn't get called on her behavior in both the manga and anime and it is Shinji who suffers for her problems. Does anyone else notice this?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 17, 2014 4:36 pm ...Or Rei pilots 01 and Shinji pilots 02. Remember he DID synch with it the episode prior, nevermind that making no sense at all. Hmm. Maybe just as Kyoko lost the part of herself that recognizes Asuka, perhaps the fragment of her soul in 02 recognizes ANYONE as Asuka? Hell if I know.
